site.title: THE O-CODEX
site.subtitle: A shared reference for the wandering company, gathered from rumor, memory, and hard-earned truth.

categories.label: Characters
categories.description: Allies, rivals, patrons, and dangerous names the party keeps hearing.

categories.label: Settlements
categories.description: Settlements, capitals, and crossroads worth remembering.

categories.label: Stories
categories.description: Threads the party can follow, revisit, or fear they have left too long.

categories.label: Manifests
categories.description: Relics, tools, and heirlooms with enough imbued magic to matter.

categories.label: Divions
categories.description: Motes of Divine power, either left for those with bravery and heart to find, or gifted to those with aspirations that reach beyond themselves.

categories.label: Summaries
categories.description: Session recaps, overviews, and high-level records for the party. The details here can be used to draw connections later...

categories.label: Domains
categories.description: Large areas that encompass wildly different climates and cultures. Your travels will take you between many of them.

entries.name: Cooper Vale
entries.summary: A soft-spoken trail guide who remembers every road that once mattered.
entries.description: Cooper works as a guide for caravans moving between the western marsh roads and the hill forts inland. He speaks gently, notices everything, and becomes hard to fool once someone lies to him. Locals trust him with routes they would never write down, especially when the old roads begin shifting in bad weather.
entries.status: Usually available in Riverwatch between caravan seasons

details.Known For: Remembering abandoned roads and smuggler cuts
details.Party Hook: Can point the group toward hidden passes or forgotten shrines

entries.name: Mirelle Thorne
entries.summary: An exhausted noble archivist trying to keep dangerous records out of the wrong hands.
entries.description: Mirelle serves the Candle Bastion archive, where she catalogs disputed histories and sealed testimonies. She is precise in speech, patient with curious people, and visibly tense whenever court politics enter the room. She often hires capable outsiders when she needs something done quietly.

details.Useful Secret: Knows which family lines were erased from public records

entries.name: Brannic Salt
entries.summary: A scarred harbor broker who trades in rumors as often as cargo.
entries.description: Brannic controls a surprising amount of dockside traffic without ever claiming to be in charge of anything. He buys stolen maps, broken relics, and information from sailors too desperate to keep it. He smiles often, but only when he believes he already knows what someone will say next.

details.Leverage: Debt ledgers and private shipping manifests

entries.name: Riverwatch
entries.summary: A trade city of flooded stairs, rope bridges, and too many secrets in the lower docks.
entries.description: Riverwatch sits where the blackwater channels widen enough for real trade. Its upper wards are built from reclaimed fort stone, while the lower wards lean over the water in cramped timber layers. Caravans, barges, smugglers, mercenaries, and pilgrims all cross paths here, which makes the city wealthy and consistently one bad week away from chaos.

details.Adventure Use: Excellent base city for recurring NPCs and side quests

entries.name: Crownhaven
entries.summary: The inland capital where polished ceremonies hide brittle politics.
entries.description: Crownhaven is the ceremonial heart of the realm, though many border folk would argue it has forgotten how the realm actually lives. The city is elegant, heavily watched, and full of old institutions that still matter because everyone fears what would happen if they stopped. Public order is strong, but influence is the true currency.

details.Ruling Presence: High Court and its attached ministries
details.Street Reputation: Beautiful, expensive, and dangerous to misread
details.Story Use: Court intrigue, records research, elite rivals

entries.name: Hollowmere
entries.summary: A marsh settlement built around peat fires, old graves, and stubborn people.
entries.description: Hollowmere survives because the people there know the marsh better than anyone who would try to conquer it. Homes cluster on raised stone foundations, and every family seems to remember a ghost story tied to the nearby barrows. Visitors are tolerated as long as they respect the water, the dead, and local warnings.

details.Exports: Bog salt, reed cloth, medicinal moss
details.Threats: Sinkways, grave robbers, pale lights over the water

entries.title: The Moonlit Oath
entries.summary: Recover a missing oathblade before two feuding knightly lines start a private war.
entries.description: A ceremonial sword vanished on the night both houses were meant to renew a truce under moonlight. Each side insists the other staged the theft, and both are close to mobilizing retainers. The party can investigate the theft, expose the real culprit, or decide whether the truth is worth the blood it may spill anyway.

details.Primary Location: Crownhaven and the Moon Court estate roads
Possible Outcomes: Keep the blade hidden for a third faction

entries.title: The Bell Below the Mire
entries.summary: The marsh bells have started ringing underground, and no one agrees on why.
entries.description: Hollowmere fishers report hearing temple bells under the peat after sundown. The sound has drawn lights over the bog, strange footprints near old graves, and a sudden burst of grave robbery in nearby barrows. The party needs to learn whether something buried is waking, calling, or simply baiting the living closer.

details.Recommended Tone: Eerie investigation that can turn into a descent
Main Clues: Priests arguing over a forbidden burial rite

entries.title: The Golden Tax
entries.summary: A royal tax convoy has vanished, and too many people want it found for the wrong reason.
entries.description: A heavily guarded convoy carrying tax silver never arrived in Crownhaven. Officials want quiet recovery, dock brokers want the route, and local rebels want proof that the treasury is weaker than it appears. Following the trail means choosing who gets the truth and who gets a lie they can live with.

Complications: The guards may have staged the disappearance
Brannic Salt may already have one chest
The silver could fund relief for frontier towns

entries.name: Lantern Reliquary
entries.summary: A bronze field lantern that reveals old ward lines and hidden shrine marks.
entries.description: This squat lantern burns with pale oil light that does not behave like ordinary flame. When opened in darkness, it traces old protective symbols across stone, roots, and thresholds. Priests claim it was once used to inspect forgotten pilgrim roads after wars left the temples in ruin.

details.Practical Use: Helps locate hidden symbols, shrines, and warded passageways
details.Drawback: Its light can also reveal the holder's presence to certain spirits
details.Current Rumor: One was seen on sale in Riverwatch for far too little

entries.name: Ashen Signet
entries.summary: A ring of blackened silver that warms when old vows are spoken nearby.
entries.description: The Ashen Signet was forged for oathkeepers who needed warning when sworn promises were being twisted or broken. It does not reveal lies directly, but it reacts to binding language, compelled bargains, and hidden terms spoken within earshot. Many courts would pay well to keep such a thing private.

details.Complication: May react to ancient vows no one meant to disturb
details.Suggested Hook: Tie it to The Moonlit Oath or a hidden succession claim

entries.name: Gravesalt Flask
entries.summary: A stoppered iron flask that keeps blessed salt dry even in cursed wetlands.
entries.description: Priests of the old road shrines used these flasks while traveling through rain, marsh, and battlefield mud. A pinch of the preserved salt can strengthen funerary rites, harden a temporary ward, or expose where the dead have been disturbed recently. It looks humble, but people who know what it is rarely ignore it.

details.Rarity: Common among old shrine keepers, scarce elsewhere
details.Perfect For: Hollowmere stories, grave sites, and haunted roads
details.Maintenance: Needs to be refilled during proper rites

entries.name: Alden
entries.summary: An arid land of inventors, engineering marvels, and a polished empire hiding rigid control beneath its order.
entries.description: Alden is packed with builders, engineers, and relentless experimenters. Though some edges are mild, most of the domain trends arid, while the southern Gialden Strip supplies a rich mountain surplus of metal shared with Giaslan. That bounty feeds Alden's obsession with construction and invention, especially in the capital of Laregola, where a powerful empire claims to keep peace among the domain's volatile creators. Beneath that orderly surface lies something colder: discipline so severe that soldiers and defenders are conditioned to suppress individuality and treat personality itself as dangerous variance. A rebel force known as the Variants resists that doctrine, with Dahlia Fausse serving as the public strategist while Kayla Fulmine remains the hidden driving force behind the movement.

details.Climate: Mostly arid, with colder southern mountains
details.Known For: Metalworking, engineering feats, and experimental creations

entries.name: Altasar
entries.summary: A wealthy grassland democracy of soul magic, respected across multiple domains for its rare mastery of the soul.
entries.description: Altasar is a broad kingdom of plains and grasslands with lush mountains and forests worked into its edges. It is famed for soul-based arts that are extraordinarily rare elsewhere: soul weaving, soulspeaking, and more flexible applications of soul that can enhance bodies, bend words, or even imitate other powers. Because so few kingdoms wield soul magic at scale, Altasar enjoys unusual prestige and has built trade and communication networks reaching several domains away. Its capital, Droland, is a democracy, and near the center of the land rises a dormant spire long believed to be a key to crossing planes of existence. Even with that mystery looming overhead, Altasar itself remains stable and strangely untouched by many disturbances that affect surrounding lands.

details.Climate: Plains, grasslands, lush mountains, and forests
details.Known For: Soul weaving, soulspeaking, and long-range communication

entries.name: Caybluff
entries.summary: A high plateau of cliffs and star-filled skies, best known for chroma sorcerers and the impossible Caybluff Fissure.
entries.description: Caybluff is a high-altitude domain of plateaus, sharp cliffs, and open skies. Its relative lack of industry and its exceptional elevation make it one of the best places in the world to study the stars, which has led to an unusually high concentration of Chroma sorcerers. On the lower eastern side lies the Caybluff Fissure, a ravine whose mystery defines the domain. Looking down reveals a horizon-like landscape rotated impossibly into view, as though a distant world were laid on its side beneath the earth. Those who have fallen into it seem to keep dropping toward that horizon without end, vanishing into a fate no one understands.

details.Terrain: Plateaus, cliffs, and elevated open land

entries.name: Cirrusia
entries.summary: A cloud-ocean sky domain of floating islands, wild beauty, and constant danger.
entries.description: Cirrusia feels less like a domain and more like a living sky. Travelers can walk on clouds, climb vast vines, and find floating islands overhead that host radically different ecologies from one another. The beauty of the place is matched by its volatility: magical beasts, unstable politics, and violent weather constantly remind visitors that Cirrusia is not a dreamland but a dangerous frontier. The floating islands are especially remarkable because they do not remain fixed. They shift individually and collectively, changing both their own wildlife and their positions in the sky as they rotate around the domain every few days.

details.Terrain: Walkable clouds, floating islands, and sky-vines
details.Known For: Natural beauty, magical beasts, and violent weather
details.Travel Hazard: Storms can make ordinary movement nearly impossible
details.Strange Feature: The islands constantly rotate around the domain

entries.name: Dynestea
entries.summary: A resource-rich land of mixed climates where prime magic and reverence for nature shape daily life.
entries.description: Dynestea sits at the crossroads of wildly different neighboring climates and seems to inherit something from all of them. Lush greenery borders Cirrusia to the south, warm deserts stretch toward Klabats in the west, the oceanic reach of Wano lies to the east, and chilly mountain country rises toward Slipway in the north. Because so many environments meet here, the domain is abundant in natural resources and exceptionally suited to prime magic. Its people hold deep respect for the earth, forests, and wildlife, and many of its practitioners specialize in bending nature itself to their will. The result is a domain that feels broad, alive, and fundamentally tied to the living world around it.

details.Climate: A mix of forest, desert, ocean influence, and mountain cold
details.Known For: Prime magic and abundance of natural resources
details.Cultural Focus: Reverence for earth, forests, and wildlife
details.Regional Strength: Nearly every major element is naturally represented

entries.name: Hemwood
entries.summary: A dim forest domain under growing heat and political strain, where tyrants argue over a hellish seal at its center.
entries.description: Hemwood is a shadowed forest region known for wizards who favor the fire aspect of prime and, more rarely, moon-based magic. Earth magic also appears among its more dangerous practitioners, giving the domain a reputation for crushing force hidden beneath its dark canopy. At its center stands a Hell Sigil and Summoning Circle tied to rising heat that has unsettled the entire region. The capital, Moonstead, was a democracy until recently, but a tyranny seized control and now uses the spreading heat as justification for drastic action. Some rulers argue that the sealed being at the center should be bound tighter; others believe it must be released and destroyed to end the threat at its source. Either way, Hemwood remains unstable, anxious, and primed for conflict.

details.Climate: Dim forestland growing hotter over time
details.Known For: Fire prime, moon magic, and brutal earth casters
details.Central Threat: The Hell Sigil and the Summoning Circle

entries.name: Laytide
entries.summary: An almost entirely aquatic domain whose history and politics are shaped by the vanished sea lord Aquiel.
entries.description: Laytide is one of the strangest domains in Maladen because so much of it is simply water. Its past was permanently marked by the sudden arrival of Aquiel, Lord of Seas, a leviathan demigod who appeared without warning and remained long enough to raise sea levels across the domain for centuries. When Aquiel vanished at the turn of the year 1000, the waters dropped just as quickly, leaving behind a culture that never stopped measuring itself against that event. As the calendar edges toward 1500, public unease has returned. Historians warn that Aquiel may come again, and even members of the royal family are divided between indifference and growing dread over what that return could mean for Laytide's future.

details.Known For: Aquiel, sea-level shifts, and ocean travel
details.Current Anxiety: Fear of Aquiel's return near the year 1500

entries.name: Klabats
entries.summary: A rough land of flow warriors, warlike tribes, and a culture that prizes breathing, battle, and bodily mastery.
entries.description: Klabats is the homeland of flow warriors, fighters who use disciplined breathing and meditation to push their physical abilities far beyond ordinary limits. In rare cases, that same flow can even magnify magic with spectacular effect. The domain lacks a single formal capital, instead scattering its identity across many kingdoms and wartribes, each with its own customs and strength. Education is not what Klabats is known for, but combat, endurance, and blunt self-belief define much of the land's character. The domain is also tied to an echo plane known as the War-torn World, a reflection of what Maladen might have become had the ancient war between Order and Chaos never ended.

details.Capital: None; many kingdoms and tribes claim local authority
details.Known For: Flow techniques and physically enhanced warriors

entries.name: Giaslan
entries.summary: A frigid mountain realm of astrologers and precise chroma magic, ruled from the heights of Chillspire.
entries.description: Giaslan is a harsh, chilly mountain domain where clear skies and impossible cold have shaped both the land and its magic. Its astrologers use Chroma with mathematical precision, drawing on constellations and horizon-light to call down devastating force. The capital, Chillspire, stretches across the mountain known as Viserion's Peak and stands as the domain's political and symbolic heart. Though Giaslan long seemed uneventful, its leaders have grown increasingly concerned with the possibility of restoring warmth to their frozen lands, even considering dangerous contact with Hemwood in search of answers. Beneath the domain's calm exterior lingers the presence of isolated dragons, icy peaks, and the whispered terror of Viserion himself.

details.Political Concern: Seeking ways to restore heat to the domain
